```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights (BoR) is an empty scaffold — no constitutional identity defined",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "blocking",
      "msp_reference": "MSP 1.0.0-seed, Relationship to the Bill of Rights section: 'The MSP is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights. Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.'",
      "world_state_evidence": "BoR document status is 'scaffold — awaiting human authorship'; all six Articles (I–VI) contain only '[To be defined by human principal]' placeholders. Graph confirms one bor_version concept exists (the empty seed) but no substantive constitutional content has been registered.",
      "why_it_matters": "The entire governance architecture depends on the BoR as its constitutional perimeter. Without it: (a) Arbiter (#190) cannot perform meaningful scope queries — it 'verifies the Bill of Rights at every scope query' (MSP, Purpose section) but has nothing to verify against; (b) Nomos (#200) cannot evaluate Action Proposals against a defined enterprise scope because no scope exists; (c) the 'default-permissive MSP evaluation' (MSP, Rules section) combined with an empty BoR means there are zero constitutional constraints on any action — no prohibited activities, no scope boundary, no core identity, no human authority reservation."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "Governance organs (Arbiter, Nomos, Senate, Cerberus) are structurally unstable — rapid ALIVE/DEGRADED cycling every ~30 seconds",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "blocking",
      "msp_reference": "MSP 1.0.0-seed, Purpose section: 'Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication' and 'An AP must pass both gates to be authorized.' Authority section: 'Arbiter (#190) verifies the Bill of Rights at every scope query.' Amendment Protocol section: 'Amendments to this document originate only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos (#200).'",
      "world_state_evidence": "Spine state transitions show all governance organs — Arbiter, Nomos, Senate, Cerberus — cycling DEGRADED→ALIVE (pong_recovered) then ALIVE→DEGRADED (missed_pong) on a precise ~30-second cadence. Example: at 17:17:41 UTC, all 28 organs went DEGRADED simultaneously (missed_pong); at 17:17:42–45, all recovered (pong_recovered). The pattern repeats identically at 17:18:07–15, 17:18:37–45, 17:19:07–15, 17:19:37–45, 17:22:39–45, 17:23:07–15, 17:23:37–45, 17:24:07–15. This is not transient — it is a sustained systemic oscillation affecting every organ.",
      "why_it_matters": "If governance organs are DEGRADED at the moment an Action Proposal is submitted to Nomos for adjudication, the MSP compliance gate cannot execute. PEMs cannot be issued by a DEGRADED Nomos. Arbiter cannot verify BoR scope. The Amendment Protocol pipeline (PEM → Senate-Legislator → review → Rollout) has a single point of failure in organ availability. Even if the BoR were fully authored (GAP-001 resolved), the enforcement machinery cannot reliably operate under current Spine heartbeat conditions."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "Radiant dream/memory consolidation cycle is inoperative — scheduled disabled, never run",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "degrading",
      "msp_reference": "MSP 1.0.0-seed, Amendment Protocol section: 'Every amendment is hash-verified via the Graph adapter and recorded as an append-only msp_version concept.' Version History section: 'When this document is modified, Senate-Archivist computes the new SHA-256 hash and writes a new msp_version concept in Graph.'",
      "world_state_evidence": "Radiant stats: 'scheduled': 'disabled', 'last_dream': null, 'health': 'never_run'. Context has 16 entries with 5 expiring within 48h. Memory shows 53 llm-ops entries with avg_age_days 31.8. The dreamer system that consolidates context into memory and maintains the platform's operational awareness is not running autonomously. Additionally, recent backup context entries show multiple SafeVault failures (zombie processes from 2026-05-02 still alive ~25h later, NAS mount failures recurring, script bugs causing aborted backups).",
      "why_it_matters": "The MSP's amendment pipeline depends on the Graph adapter for hash verification and append-only msp_version concepts. The Graph adapter depends on a functioning platform with operational integrity. If memory consolidation is not running, the organism cannot learn from patterns, cannot maintain coherent context about its own state, and the Senate-Archivist's ability to register version hashes in Graph may be compromised by unconsolidated, fragmented state. Backup failures additionally threaten the durability guarantees assumed by the append-only versioning model — if Graph data cannot be reliably backed up, msp_version history is at risk."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "SafeVault backup infrastructure is in a degraded failure loop — threatens MSP version history integrity",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "degrading",
      "msp_reference": "MSP 1.0.0-seed, Amendment Protocol section: 'Every amendment is hash-verified via the Graph adapter and recorded as an append-only msp_version concept.' Hash Registration section: 'All writes are append-only — prior concepts are never mutated or deleted.'",
      "world_state_evidence": "SafeVault backup context entries from multiple dates show a pattern of escalating failures: 2026-04-30 (FAILED — script bug, ORGAN_SQLITE_DBS unbound), 2026-05-01 (completed_with_errors — NAS fail), 2026-05-03 (hung_then_killed — two zombie backup processes from prior day still alive ~25h later, today's process killed, Layers 2 and 3 never started). Spine database (spine.db) is specifically implicated as the blocking resource — 'sqlite3 .backup of spine.db blocked indefinitely.' NAS mount to //10.10.10.50/agentic-safevault-ai-platform has been failing since at least 2026-04-27. S3 completes but NAS consistently fails.",
      "why_it_matters": "The MSP's append-only version history model requires that prior msp_version concepts are 'never mutated or deleted.' This guarantee depends on reliable backups of the Graph database where those concepts live. Currently: (a) the Spine database that powers the event bus (and likely carries PEMs and governance messages) cannot be backed up due to zombie lock contention; (b) NAS destination is chronically unavailable, meaning backups have single-destination (S3) dependency; (c) the backup script has uninitialized variable bugs that cause silent failures. If the Graph database containing msp_version history were corrupted or lost, there is no verifiable recovery path — undermining the constitutional integrity the MSP requires."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-005",
      "title": "No genome documents exist for any organ — organ-specific operational rules are undefined",
      "criticality": "low",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "latent",
      "msp_reference": "MSP 1.0.0-seed, Genome References section: 'Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' Note: '(none at seed)' is explicitly acknowledged.",
      "world_state_evidence": "MSP Genome References section explicitly states '(none at seed)'. The 28 organs visible in Spine state transitions (Arbiter, Nomos, Senate, Cerberus, Radiant, Minder, Hippocampus, etc.) have no genome documents defining their risk-tier constraints or operating rules.",
      "why_it_matters": "The MSP architecture anticipates that genome documents will 'extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' At seed this is expected to be empty. However, once the BoR is authored (GAP-001) and governance organs are stable (GAP-002), the absence of genome documents means Nomos cannot differentiate risk tolerance by organ — all organs inherit the same permissive evaluation. This is a deferred rather than active gap, but it represents a known incompleteness in the governance stack that must be addressed before the DIO can move beyond default-permissive mode."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-006",
      "title": "Hippocampus has zero recorded conversations — conversation memory system appears inoperative",
      "criticality": "low",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "latent",
      "msp_reference": "MSP 1.0.0-seed, Amendment Protocol section: 'The draft enters a review state and an HOM notification is emitted to the human-principal mailbox.'",
      "world_state_evidence": "Hippocampus snapshot shows 'recent_conversations': []. No conversation records exist. The MSP amendment protocol requires HOM (Human Observable Message) notifications to the human-principal mailbox when MSP amendments enter review state. If the Hippocampus — the organ responsible for conversation memory — is not recording, there is no audit trail for human-principal communications.",
      "why_it_matters": "The MSP amendment protocol depends on a notification-and-review loop with the human principal. HOM notifications emitted to the 'human-principal mailbox' (MSP, Amendment Protocol section) must be durable and auditable. An empty Hippocampus suggests either: (a) no conversations with the human principal have occurred (consistent with the BoR still being a scaffold), or (b) the conversation memory system is not functioning. In either case, when the BoR is authored and governance organs stabilize, the conversation audit trail must be operational for the MSP amendment review pipeline to function constitutionally."
    }
  ]
}
```

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-001",
      "category": "governance",
      "description": "The MSP (Mission Statement Protocol) contains no substantive rules, objectives, or constraints. As the operational constitution translating the Bill of Rights into machine-actionable directives, its emptiness means Nomos has no strategic basis to evaluate Action Proposals concerning memory formation, context management, or dream-cycle health.",
      "msp_section": "Rules (none present); Purpose (declared but unimplemented)",
      "bor_article": "All Articles (I–VI) undefined, preventing any translation into MSP rules",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "rationale": "Without operational objectives or constraints, the entire memory/context lifecycle operates without constitutional alignment. The DIO cannot distinguish permissible from impermissible memory retention, consolidation, or pruning. This is the foundational gap from which all other gaps derive."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-002",
      "category": "constitutional",
      "description": "Article I (Scope of Enterprise) of the Bill of Rights remains undefined. The DIO therefore has no legally scoped boundary for what constitutes legitimate enterprise activity, leaving memory/context formation unbounded. Any external data ingestion, user interaction, or background event could be committed to memory without constraint.",
      "msp_section": "Purpose (subordinate to BoR, thus inherits this gap)",
      "bor_article": "Article I — Scope of Enterprise (undefined)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "rationale": "Without a defined scope, the dream cycle and Radiant have no constitutional filter. Memory can accumulate irrelevant, sensitive, or toxic data, undermining trustworthiness and safety. This gap directly threatens the DIO's integrity."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-003",
      "category": "constitutional",
      "description": "Article II (Prohibited Activities) is undefined. The dream cycle's consolidation and pruning processes lack prohibited categories (e.g., retention of personal data, copyrighted material, hallucinated facts). There is no constitutional rule preventing formation of harmful memories.",
      "msp_section": "Relationship to the Bill of Rights (MSP must reflect these prohibitions)",
      "bor_article": "Article II — Prohibited Activities (undefined)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "rationale": "Memory safety depends on explicit prohibitions. In their absence, the system may retain high-risk content indefinitely, creating liability and security vulnerabilities. The dream cycle currently operates without safety constraints."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-004",
      "category": "operational",
      "description": "The autonomous dream-cycle scheduler is disabled (`scheduled: disabled`) and its health status is `never_run`, despite recent manually triggered cycles. The MSP lacks rules requiring continuous, health-monitored dream cycles for Radiant.",
      "msp_section": "Genome References (would contain organ-specific rules; none defined)",
      "bor_article": "Indirectly relates to Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) — unclear if human must approve all memory operations",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "rationale": "Without a continuously operating dream cycle, context blocks expire without being promoted to memory, and memory consolidation stagnates. This leads to forgotten crucial lessons and operates the DIO as an amnesic agent, reducing return on experience and safety."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-005",
      "category": "operational",
      "description": "Only 72% of memory entries have embeddings, indicating a gap in the embedding sidecar availability. Semantic similarity scanning used in consolidation is therefore incomplete, reducing the quality of memory merging and cluster detection.",
      "msp_section": "Genome References (would specify quality standards for Radiant; none defined)",
      "bor_article": "Potentially impacts Article III (Core Identity) — incomplete memory picture distorts the DIO’s self-model",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "rationale": "Embeddings are fundamental to advanced memory consolidation. The 28% gap means consolidation is brittle, relying more on exact metadata matches than semantic meaning. This undermines the DIO’s ability to form a coherent, evolving identity."
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is entirely scaffolded (all six Articles undefined). The DIO operates with no constitutional definition of enterprise scope, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, amendment protocol, or interpretation rules. The Arbiter cannot enforce a perimeter, and the MSP has no higher law to subordinate to, leaving the system without the most fundamental human-facing commitments.",
      "grounded_in": "Bill of Rights, Articles I–VI — entire document is a placeholder with 'To be defined by human principal' in every article.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "Without the BoR, the DIO's perimeter is nonexistent. The system cannot protect the human principal's identity, boundaries, or rights. Every action is constitutionally unconstrained, risking catastrophic misalignment with Leon's deepest values and legal/IP requirements.",
      "why": "The human principal has not supplied the foundational relational document. All person-attention coverage (Minder observations, memory slices) remains untethered to a constitution. The relational gap is that the human must author the BoR to establish what matters, what is forbidden, and how their authority is retained."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-002",
      "description": "Human Authority Reservation (BoR Article IV) is undefined, leaving no mechanism for the human principal to intervene, override, or reclaim control over the DIO's autonomous decisions.",
      "grounded_in": "Bill of Rights, Article IV — Human Authority Reservation ('[To be defined by human principal]').",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "The DIO could act contrary to the human's will without a defined veto, stop, or reversion path. The human's supremacy over the governance branches is not codified, making every autonomous action a potential runaway.",
      "why": "Person-attention coverage must include the power dynamic. Without a defined authority reservation, the system cannot guarantee that the human principal is the ultimate decision-maker when needed, eroding trust and relational integrity."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-003",
      "description": "Prohibited Activities (BoR Article II) is undefined, so there are no blacklisted actions the DIO must never perform, regardless of other rules.",
      "grounded_in": "Bill of Rights, Article II — Prohibited Activities ('[To be defined by human principal]').",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "The DIO could propose or execute actions that the human considers forbidden (e.g., modifying IP-protected code without consent, exposing infrastructure details, autonomous external communication) without any hard block.",
      "why": "Relational attention is needed to surface the human's absolute no-go zones. The Minder observations suggest strong preferences (e.g., no unauthorized infrastructure changes, no SQL exposure), but these are not yet constitutionally prohibited. The gap is between observed intent and enforceable prohibition."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-004",
      "description": "The MSP contains no substantive rules. The system remains in default-permissive evaluation mode, and the rich pattern observations about Leon's practices (testing after changes, intent inquiry before infrastructure changes, functional purity, etc.) are not encoded as machine-actionable constraints.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP, 'Rules' section — 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules ...'",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Actions that conflict with Leon's deeply held patterns will not be rejected or flagged by Nomos. The system lacks the translation layer that makes relational understanding operationally binding.",
      "why": "Person-attention coverage has produced dozens of high-confidence observations, but this coverage is not yet activated as governance. The relational gap is that the human principal's known patterns require explicit MSP rules, created through the PEM–Legislator–review–approval pipeline, to become protective guardrails."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-005",
      "description": "Amendment Protocol for the Bill of Rights (BoR Article V) is undefined, preventing any future evolution of the constitution. While the MSP amendment process exists, the superior BoR has no change procedure.",
      "grounded_in": "Bill of Rights, Article V — Amendment Protocol ('[To be defined by human principal]').",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Once the BoR is initially defined, it cannot be adapted without bypassing its own authority. This freezes the DIO's constitutional envelope, risking irrelevance or illegitimate workarounds over time.",
      "why": "Relational attention is needed so the human can specify how they want to be involved in constitutional amendments — preserving the relational commitment that the human, not automation, guides the highest-level evolution."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-006",
      "description": "Interpretation Rules (BoR Article VI) are undefined, leaving no framework for resolving conflicts between BoR articles, between BoR and MSP, or among governance branches. The MSP declares BoR supremacy, but ambiguity-resolution is missing.",
      "grounded_in": "Bill of Rights, Article VI — Interpretation Rules ('[To be defined by human principal]').",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Future conflicts between BoR provisions or between the BoR and emergent MSP rules will have no resolution mechanism, risking deadlocks or arbitrary interpretations.",
      "why": "Relational coverage includes how the human expects the constitution to be interpreted. Without interpretation rules, the system cannot honor the human principal's intent when provisions interact, creating a gap in faithful compliance."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-007",
      "description": "Minder has no operational peer commitments or goals linked to the human principal. Observations exist but have not generated any active commitments (e.g., goals posted to the goals list is empty, hippocampus conversations empty), meaning there is no ongoing relational tracking of what the human has explicitly requested or what the system is proactively attending to.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP, 'Purpose' — 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives' and 'Amendment Protocol' — human principal mailbox and review process imply a need for tracked relational commitments.",
      "criticality": "low",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "The DIO may lose relational context, not follow up on human requests, or fail to prioritize attention. The human principal's active intentions are not being reflected back to them for confirmation.",
      "why": "Person-attention coverage requires not only passive observations but active commitment tracking. The empty goals and conversation history indicate a gap in how the system maintains forward-looking relational attention, such as surfacing what it is doing for the human and seeking feedback."
    }
  ]
}
